item.title: GCP CMEK and CSEK
item.description: Overview Customer Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) Key created and managed with Cloud KMS Assigned to resource Alternative to Google managed keys Not necessarily more secure: Incurs more costs than Google managed Can control lifecycle of keys Customer Supplied Encryption Keys (CSEK) Customer specifies contents of key material Keys stored on-prem, or in external service Compatibale with Cloud Storage and Compute Engine References Cloud KMS GCP Services

item.title: GCP Decision Trees
item.description: Compute Services Decision Tree Storage Databases Load Balancing External Internal Big Data Key Management References GCP Services

item.title: Cloud Armor
item.description: Overview DDoS protection WAF Automatically enabled for HTTP(S), SSL Proxy and TCP Proxy load balancers Mitigates OWASP’s top 10 list, e.

item.title: Cloud Data Loss Prevention
item.description: Overview Protect sensitive data Inspection, classification, de-idenitifcation of data 150+ data detectors—PII Inputs: Data strams Structured text Cloud Storage files BigQuery Images Export to CSCC, Data Catalog or external SIEMs References Cloud Security Command Center GCP Services

item.title: Cloud EKM
item.description: Overview Cloud External Key Manager Keys from external key management partners Works only with services which support customer managed encryption keys (CMEKs), e.

item.title: Cloud HSM
item.description: Overview Hardware security modules Hosts keys and performs actions in a cluster of FIPS 140-2 level 3 certified devices Compliance with hardware requirements Keys bound to region—support for multi-region Keys non-exportable Tamper resistant, with evidence of tamper Horizontal autoscaling References GCP Services

item.title: Cloud IAM
item.description: Overview Who Who is allowed access to resources Principals / members: Google accounts Service accounts Google Groups Google Workspace accounts Cloud Identity domains All authenticated users All users Access What access principals have Roles: Groups of permissions Can’t assign individual permissions to principals—must assign a role Security Admin Role—role which allows management of any IAM policy Which Which resources a principal can access e.

item.title: Cloud Interconnect
item.description: Overview Extend on-prem network to GCP VPC Dedicated Interconnect Partner Interconnect Direct physical connection to GCP Connect via intermediary High bandwidth needs Lower bandwidth needs 10 Gbps or 100 Gbps circuits Depends on partner capabilities 50 Mbps – 50 Gbps capacities 50 Mbps – 50 Gbps capacities Traffic not encrypted (application layer optional) Traffic not encrypted (application layer optional) Cloud VPN not supported Cloud VPN not supported References GCP Services

item.title: Cloud KMS
item.description: Overview Generate, manage and apply encryption keys HA, low latency Encrypt and decrypt only—does not store secrets Access control to keys Supports: Symmetrical keys (e.

item.title: Cloud Router
item.description: Overview Dynamic routing Supports: Cloud VPN Dedicated Interconnect Links external network to VPC via BGP Automatically learns subnets in VPC—announces to on-prem network Works with router appliances for high performance References GCP Services

item.title: Cloud Security Command Center
item.description: Overview Security management and risk platform Prevent, detect, respond Integrate and monitor many security services on GCP—also external services Identity compliance violations/misconfigurations, e.

item.title: Cloud VPN
item.description: Overview Traffic encrypted between client and GCP by IPsec gateways Static IP required—dynamic “dial-in” connections not supported Best practices: Keep Cloud VPN in its own project Use dynamic routing and BGP Apply secure firewall rules Use strong pre-shared keys References GCP Services

item.title: Event Threat Detection
item.description: Overview CSCC premium feature Analyse logs from Cloud Logging in near real-time VPCs flow logs Firewall logs Cloud NAT logs DNS logs Linux system logs 25+ rules, e.

item.title: GCP Resource Manager
item.description: Overview Organises projects into custom folder hierarchies Allows for modification of IAM policies across the organization Cloud Asset Inventory Monitor and analyze all assets, including IAM policies Organization Policy Service Constrains on resources—guardrails Keep the organization in compliance References GCP Services

item.title: Google Cloud Identity
item.description: Overview Fully managed identity-as-a-service Each user and group is given a Cloud Identity Account Allows cloud IAM to manage access Allows federation with other identity providers (e.

item.title: Identity Aware Proxy
item.description: Overview IAP Central authorization layer for HTTPS applications Secure resources with authorization and authentication—enforces policies No VPN required Works with GCP load balancers and IAM—only authenticated requests allowed Works with App Engine, Compute Engine, Kubernetes Engine, Cloud Run and on-prem References GCP Services

item.title: Secret Manager
item.description: Overview Store, manage, access secrets as blobs or strings Passwords, API keys, TLS certs etc. Data immutible New versions created with each modification Best practices Principle of least privilege Limit access with IAM Use Secret Manager API instead of environment variables Reference secrets by version number References GCP Services

item.title: Web Security Scanner
item.description: Overview Detects vulnerabilities in: App Engine Compute Engine Kubernetes Engine Crawler-based—crawls public URLs and IPs not behind firewalls CSCC standard—custom manual scans CSCC premium—managed scans (defaults to weekly) Examples: XSS Flash injection Mixed content (HTTP + HTTPS) Insecure JavaScript libraries Readable passwords References Cloud Security Command Center GCP Services

item.title: Cloud CDN
item.description: Overview Serve content closer to users Works with external HTTP(S) load balancers HTTP caching—controlled with cache control header, or automatic caching of static content Sources: Instance groups Zonal NEGs Serverless NEGs (e.

item.title: Cloud DNS
item.description: Overview Global, scalable, fully managed 100% uptime Public and private DNS zones Features: Access managed via IAM Integrated with Logging DNS peering and forwarding Anycast: Multiple machines sharing IP User routed to the closest machine DNSSEC References GCP Services

item.title: Cloud Domains
item.description: Overview Global domain registrar—uses Google Domains Use built-in DNS or custom nameservers DNSSEC and private Whois Features: Managed via GCP project—billing Auto domain verification with other Google services Access managed via IAM References GCP Services

item.title: GCP Static IP Addresses
item.description: Static External IP Addresses No charge for static external IPs—except for IPs reserved but not used Regional Can attach to zonal and regional resources, e.

item.title: GCP VPCs
item.description: Overview Global Single VPC for multiple regions No traffic across public internet IPv4, unicast Software defined—instant updates Automatic or custom modes, for creation of: Subnets Firewall rules Routes VPCs—global Subnets—regional Options: Shared VPC VPC peering—services available across different VPCs in different orgs and projects Bring-your-own IPs Packet mirroring—traffic analysis in Monitoring VPC Peering Transitive peering not allowed—resources in VPC A, peered with VPC B, can’t access resources in other VPCs also peered with B: References GCP Services

item.title: Cloud Bigtable
item.description: Overview Large analytical/operational workloads Key-value store High read/write rates, low latency Tables stored in instances Up to four nodes in different zones Use cases: Time-series data Marketing/financial data IoT data References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: Cloud Firestore
item.description: Overview Document store Live synchronization and offline mode Multi-user, collaborative apps Supports Datastore DBs and APIs (previous generation of Firestore) Use-case: Live asset/activity tracking Real-time analytics Media/product catalogues Social user profiles Gaming leaderboards References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: Cloud Spanner
item.description: Overview Relational—SQL 99.999% availability (5 nines) Unlimited scale For each instance, define: Configuration Compute capacity Auto sharding External consistency Backup/restore, point-in-time recovery (PITR) Use query parameters to increase efficiency and reduce cost References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: Cloud SQL
item.description: Overview Relational database Supports: SQL Server MySQL PostreSQL Auto replication, failover, backup, and point-in-time recovery (PITR)—built in HA Auto scales to 30 TB Scales manually to 96 cores / 624 GB RAM Read replicas available References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: Datastream
item.description: Overview Serverless change data capture (CDC) and replication service Synchronize data across databases and applications References GCP Services

item.title: Firebase
item.description: Overview NoSQL database Store and synchronize data Collaboration amongst users and devices in real time Mobile apps use-case References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: GCP Compute Autoscaling
item.description: Compute Autoscaling Service Min instances Min instances for HA How? Speed Compute Engine 1 instance 2 instances MIG / MIB + load balancer Moderate / better GKE Pods 1 Pod 2 Pods (e.

item.title: GCP Services
item.description: Decision Trees GCP Decision Trees Compute Compute Engine Kubernetes Engine (GKE) App Engine Cloud Run Cloud Functions Storage Local SSDs Persistent Disks Cloud Filestore Cloud Storage Databases Cloud SQL Cloud Spanner Cloud Bigtable Cloud Firestore Firebase Memorystore Datastream Networking External Networking Cloud Domains Cloud DNS Static IP Addresses Load Balancing Cloud CDN Internal Networking VPCs Cloud Interconnect Cloud VPN Cloud Router Network Endpoint Groups Big Data IoT Core Pub/Sub BigQuery Dataprep Dataproc Dataflow AI/ML Vertex AI Security Cloud IAM GCP Resource Manager Google Cloud Identity Identity Aware Proxy Cloud Security Command Center Web Security Scanner Cloud Armor Event Threat Detection Cloud Data Loss Prevention Cloud KMS CMEK and CSEK Cloud HSM Cloud EKM Secret Manager

item.title: Memorystore
item.description: Overview In memory database Redis Memcached Low latency, high throughput Use cases: Cached data References GCP Services

item.title: Anthos on Bare Metal
item.description: Overview Install Anthos clusters directly on your own hardware Use of specialized hardware Use existing data centres Anthos manages app deployment and health More efficient operations Customized security Deployment Models Admin clusters Create, modify, manage and delete user clusters User clusters Run workloads Standalone Single cluster—both and admin and user cluster in one Best for: Edge locations Single teams and workload types Multi-Cluster Admin cluster plus one or more user clusters Best for: Team separation Environment separation Hybrid Admin cluster plus one or more user clusters Admin cluster also runs user workloads Created from pre-existing standalone clusters No security concerns about workloads running on the admin cluster Operating Connect—component which associates Anthos on Bare Metal clusters with GCP Allows health of clusters to be visiable in Cloud Console References Anthos

item.title: Anthos On-Prem Storage
item.description: Overview Block/file storage is exposed to Anthos clusters via the Container Storage Interface (CSI) This is required for PersistentVolumes on Anthos on Bare Metal (and VMware—although the choice here might be easier) Google partners have developed CSI drivers that have been verified against Anthos: Dell EMC Hitachi HPE NetApp Portworx Pure Storage Robin.

item.title: Cloud Filestore
item.description: Overview File-based storage NAS Provision in zone—access via VPC using NFSv3 Use case: lift-and-shift migrations Read-only snapshots supported Tiers Tier Capacities Use cases Basic HDD: 1 – 63.

item.title: ConfigMap
item.description: Overview Non-sensitive configuration artifacts Separate configuration from workloads References Kubernetes

item.title: DaemonSet
item.description: Overview Used for background tasks—no user intervention Creates one Pod per node Automatically creates a new Pod for each node that is added to the cluster References Kubernetes

item.title: Deployment
item.description: Overview Best for stateless apps i.e. uses ReadOnlyMany or ReadWriteMany volumes only References Kubernetes

item.title: GCP Local SSDs
item.description: Overview Fastest block storage solution Attached directly to Compute Engine VMs Zonal 375 GB disks Expandable to 3, 6 or 9 TBs—combined into a single logical volume IOPs increase with disk size Data encrypted at rest Data lost when VM stops Best for transient data—media rendering, caches, analytics Can survive live migration References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: GCP Persistent Disks
item.description: Overview Disks independent of Compute Engine instance Data available ofter VM is shutdown Durable—data distributed across physical disks (99.9999%) Secure—encrypted at rest and transit Zonal Vs Regional Persistent Disks Zonal Regional Single Zone Two zones in same region Standard, balanced, SSD, extreme Standard, balanced, SSD Snapshots and boot disks Snapshots only Can resize capacity, throughput and IOPs Can resize capacity only References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
item.description: Overview Scales Pods based on metrics CPU, memory, disk usage Custom metrics—internal to the cluster External metrics—e.g., from monitoring or Pub/Sub Should be applied to the Deployment rather than the ReplicaSet Otherwise would be removed during rolling updates References Kubernetes

item.title: Ingress
item.description: Overview Exposes Kubernetes services outside the cluster GKE Ingress Implementation of the Kubernetes Ingress resource in GKE Backed by the HTTP(S) Load Balancer Internal of external References Kubernetes

item.title: Pod
item.description: Overview Fundamental Kubernetes workload abstraction Groups of containers Requests and Limits Specify requests and limits for each container in the Pod, e.

item.title: StatefulSet
item.description: Overview Each Pod receives and unique ID and hostname Stateful data stored in a PVC associated with each instance References Kubernetes

item.title: GCP Load Balancing
item.description: Overview Software defined No hardware—reacts instantly, handles spikes with no warm-up Layer 4 and layer 7 Integrates with Cloud CDN Regional Load Balancing IPv4 Health checks Session affinity Backends in one region Compliance use case—traffic stays within region Global Load Balancing IPv4 and IPv6 Multi-region failover Connects clients to closest region for lower latency Backends distributed across regions References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: App Engine
item.description: Overview PaaS serverless Regional No server management/config Comes in two flavours: App Engine Standard—first generation App Engine Flexible—second generation App Engine Standard Instantaneous scaling Scales to zero Runtimes: Python 3 Java 11 Node.

item.title: BigQuery
item.description: Overview Data warehouse Serverless Multi-regional SQL column store Terabytes to petabytes ML and BI Real-time analytics Automatic replication, 7 day storage of changes References AI ML Lifecycle GCP Services

item.title: Cloud Functions
item.description: Overview FaaS Event driven Triggers: HTTP Cloud Storage Cloud Pub/Sub Cloud Firestore Audit logs Cloud Scheduler The glue between services—both in and outside GCP Good for streaming data and IoT References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: Cloud Run
item.description: Overview Regional Fully managed PaaS Containerized applications Knative-based Any language/library/binary Autoscaling from zero References GCP Decision Trees GCP Services

item.title: Cloud Storage
item.description: Overview Infinitely scalable Highly durable (99.9999999%—eleven 9s) Unstructured data—images, videos etc. Data stored in buckets—regional or multi-regional Folder structure Object versioning—mutable files Permissions: bucket or object level Lifecycle management Rules to move objects between storage classes, or delete Storage Classes Class Usage Standard Most frequency accessed data Nearline Data accessed once per month Coldline Data accessed once per quarter Archive Data accessed once per year or less Versioning Get the current versioning state:

item.title: Compute Engine
item.description: Overview Highly configurable Zonal Choice of machine types: General purpose Compute optimized Memory optimized Processor optimized (GPUs) Public or private boot disk images Preemptible VMs Can stop at any time Will definitely stop after 24 hrs 60 – 90% cheaper than standard VMs Sole Tenant Nodes Dedicated server Useful for specific hardware requirements, e.

item.title: Dataflow
item.description: Overview Serverless—managed service, horizontal scaling Batch and streaming with a single model Often not the case Apache Beam Open source Beam SDK—Java, Python, Go (not currently supported) Dataflow is the backend for the Beam API References GCP Decision Trees Data Lifecycle AI ML Lifecycle GCP Services

item.title: Dataprep
item.description: Overview Explore, clean, prepare for analysis and ML Can detect schemas—data types, joins, anomalies Prediction of next transformation Transformation functions, e.

item.title: Dataproc
item.description: Overview Map/Reduce—Apache Spark and Hadoop Backed by Zonal VMs Can use preemptible VMs—reduce cost One VM must be non-preemptible References GCP Decision Trees Data Lifecycle AI ML Lifecycle GCP Services

item.title: GCP AI ML Services
item.description: The AI/ML Lifecycle

item.title: GCP ML Best Practices
item.description: Environment Setup Use Jupyter notebooks for development One instance per teammate per project Store prepared data and model in the same project Development Prepare a good amount of training data More accurate models Store tabular data in BigQuery Store unstructured data in Cloud Storage Processing Use TensorFlow Extended for TensorFlow projects Process tabular data BigQuery Process unstructured data with: Dataflow—General use cases, Apache Bean Dataproc—Apache Hadoop or Spark use cases Link data to model with managed datasets Production Specify hardware appropriate for model CPUs vs GPUs Plan for additional inputs to model Enable autoscaling References Google Vertex AI

item.title: IoT Core
item.description: Overview Fully managed—connect, manage, ingest device data Global Device Manager Identity Authentication Configuration Control Protocol Bridge Incoming telemetry data to Pub/Sub HTTPS or MQTT Two-way comms Updates on and offline References GCP Services

item.title: Kubernetes Engine
item.description: Overview Two modes: Standard—total control Autopilot—fully managed Auto-repair and upgrade Autoscaling Clusters At least one control plane and multiple worker nodes Zonal—one control plane node Regional—multiple control plane nodes Can’t switch from regional to zonal (or vice versa) once cluster is created Private clusters—VPC native, internal IPs only For HA use multi-zonal node pools Networking Routing options: Routes-based Custom static routes VPC-native Alias IP ranges Scale better Needed for private clusters Pods routable within cluster’s VPC, and other networks connected via VPC peering Also generally routable from on-prem via VPN/Interconnect Pod IP addresses reserved before Pods created in the cluster—prevents conflicts Don’t consume custom routes quota Can apply firewall rules to Pod IPs Workload Identity Links Kubernetes Service Accounts with GCP Service Accounts—allows access to other GCP resources from within the cluster Operations Integrated with Cloud Monitoring and Logging Metrics: System metrics—low-level, e.

item.title: Pub/Sub
item.description: Overview Global messaging—consistent latency globally Scalable, durable Topic-based At-least-once model Push and pull modes Exactly once processing Messages can be ordered and filtered References GCP Services

item.title: Vertex AI
item.description: Vision API Vision detection Labelling Face / landmark recognition OCR Explicit content tagging Video Intelligence API Annotate videos From GCS, live streams or local videos Contextual info From single frame to entire video Recognise text Track objects Recognise people Translation API 100+ language translation pairs Language detection Basic or advanced versions Advanced—batch requests, Auto ML custom models Text-to-Speech and Speech-to-Text APIs Fast text-to-speech and vice versa Natural Language API Text analysis—insights from documents (e.

item.title: TerramEarth Case Study
item.description: Non-italicised points represent notes taken from Google’s official case study documentation, with keywords highlighted in bold. Points in italics represent additional insights and possible solutions.

item.title: Mountkirk Games Case Study
item.description: Non-italicised points represent notes taken from Google’s official case study documentation, with keywords highlighted in bold. Points in italics represent additional insights and possible solutions.

item.title: Helicopter Racing League Case Study
item.description: Non-italicised points represent notes taken from Google’s official case study documentation, with keywords highlighted in bold. Points in italics represent additional insights and possible solutions.

item.title: Anthos
item.description: Overview “Write once, run anywhere” Kubernetes on GCP, AWS, Azure and on-prem (VMware and bare-metal) Google managed control plane Supports: GKE clusters Cloud Run Compute Engine VMs Migrate for Anthos—migrate legacy workloads to containers Anthos Config Management Policy enforcement Guardrails Security in depth Anthos Service Mesh Managed, observable, secure communications between microservices Istio Control-plane and data-plane Traffic monitored through a proxy—auto-instrumentation/injection, so no code changes required Canary and blue/green deployments Load balancing between services Telemetry Cloud Run for Anthos Knative Serverless workloads Advanced autoscaling—scale to zero (or minimum availability) Automatic networking A/B tests—traffic splitting, rollbacks Anthos Fleets Groups of clusters Normalisation of resources, e.

item.title: Multi Cluster Ingress
item.description: Overview Google hosted service to load balance requests across GKE clusters and regions. The MultiClusterIngress Kubernetes resource is a controller for External HTTP(S) Load Balancers.

item.title: Network Endpoint Groups
item.description: Overview Collections of IPs or IP and port comninations in a single subnet References GCP Services

item.title: Compliance
item.description: Key Compliance Areas Legislation US healthcare: HIPAA Need Business Associate Addendum from GCP Commercial, e.

item.title: Data Lifecycle
item.description: The Data Lifecycle References Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect

item.title: EHR Healthcare Case Study
item.description: Non-italicised points represent notes taken from Google’s official case study documentation, with keywords highlighted in bold. Points in italics represent additional insights and possible solutions.

item.title: GCP Annotations
item.description: Security Marks For compliance purposes Adds business context to assets Adds additional layer of security Applied in the Cloud Security Command Center (CSCC) CSCC also works with labels and network tags Organization, project, or resource level Labels Key-value pairs Use cases: Attribute resources to teams or cost-centres Identify environments Cost/billing allocation Group resources to monitoring and analytics by operations Can apply to projects, but not folders Network Tags Applied to VM instances Used in firewall rules and network routes Flow Chart References Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect

item.title: GKE Cluster Autoscaling
item.description: Scaling Down Clusters can be prevented from scaling down, and deleting nodes due to: Pod affinity/anti-affinity rules preventing them from being evicted Pods with local storage Pods not managed by a controller, such as a Deployment, StatefulSet etc.

item.title: Job
item.description: For running batch workloads Each Job spins up one or more Pods to process the work Can be run in serial or parallel Often pulling work items from a queue Parallel Patterns Queue with Pod per work item Use to process all work items at once Set .

item.title: Kubernetes
item.description: Resources Pod ReplicaSet Deployment StatefulSet DaemonSet Job CronJob Service Ingress ConfigMap Secret HorizontalPodAutoscaler References Kubernetes Engine

item.title: Cloud Architecture
item.description: Definition For Google: Enable orgs to leverage GCP technologies Design/develop solutions for business requirements: Robust Secure Scalable High availability Dynamic Understand software development methodologies:

item.title: Common TCP Ports
item.description: Common Ports to Remember 80 HTTP 443 HTTPS 22 SSH 25 SMTP 53 DNS 3306 MySQL References Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect

item.title: GCP Data Lifecycle
item.description: Ingest Stream Pub/Sub Batch Cloud Storage Storage Transfer Service—moving large amounts of data from on-prem BigQuery BigQuery Transfer Service—moving from SaaS e.

item.title: GCP PCA Exam Guide
item.description: PCA’s need to: Design and plan cloud solution architectures Business focussed Understand technical requirements Networking, storage, compute Migration planning Plan for future improvements Manage and configure solution infrastructure Network topologies Data storage Compute—data processing Design for security and compliance Analysing and optimizing technical and business processes Managing implementation References Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect] Professional Cloud Architect Certification Exam Guide

item.title: Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect
item.description: Key Fundamentals To Know Distributed systems OSI Layers TCP/IP How it works IPv4 and IPV6 Public vs private RFC1918 addressing SSL/TLS SSH Common TCP Ports HTTP/S URLs and URIs Verbs Caching CORS HTTP Status Codes Site Reliability Engineering SLIs, SLOs and SLAs Case Studies EHR Healthcare Helicopter Racing League Mountkirk Games TerramEarth

item.title: HTTP Status Codes
item.description: 100s - Information 100 Continue 101 Switching protocol 200s - Success 200 OK 201 Created 202 Accepted 204 Not content 206 Partial content 300s - Redirection 301 Moved permanently 304 Not modified 307 Temporary redirect 308 Permanent redirect 400s - Client Errors 400 Bad request 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Not found 408 Request timeout 429 Too many requests 500s - Server Errors 500 Internal server error 501 Not implemented 502 Bad gateway 503 Unavailable 504 Gateway timeout 511 Network authentication required References Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect

item.title: OSI Layers
item.description: References Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect

item.title: Site Reliability Engineering
item.description: SRE is what happens when a software engineer is tasked with what used to be called operations. SLIs Service level indicators Metrics over time Specific to a user journey Examples: Request latency Failure rate Batch throughput SLOs Service level objectives Target Don’t strive for 100% Expensive Complex Not actually needed by users Use remaining time to create new features SLAs Service level agreements Contractual obligations Penalties if not met References Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect
